dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c11: H(r) = 8,537×exp(-r/8,619) km (05-model.txt L16); Domain 4 g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg)) with λg = 8,619 km (08-live-power.txt L22, L70); Domain 19 "Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92 (Sub-terrestrial structure)" (L152) with one-sided Anomaly <20 mGal threshold (L127-128). Linear coupling at the rim gives g_rim/g_pole = 837/8,537 = 0.098 → 90% drop; sqrt gives 69%; H^(1/5) gives 38% drop. New refraction-cascade paragraph adds the H(15,500-20,015 km) range = 1,400-840 km, confirming a 6-10× column-height collapse arc that any monotonic H↔g coupling forces a 5-10×-class gravity drop across, vs WGS84 ≤0.063% measured variation 70-90°S.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 4 coefficients are WGS84 tautology at r=0; the 0.5% variation comes from the WGS84 gravity-flattening k=0.005307, not from dome geometry. Domain 19 is a correlation statement plus one-sided threshold (Bouguer anomaly trivially exceeds 20 mGal in East Antarctica — pass by construction, not by prediction).
dome_code_analysis.data_sources_used: Raw-text snapshots only (08-live-power.txt, 05-model.txt, 02-wins.txt). No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ as of this cycle (see H4 c12).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 19 one-sided threshold (Anomaly <20 mGal) cannot fail when |observed| ≥ 20 mGal (East Antarctic Bouguer is -40 to -60 mGal). Domain 4 falsification criterion (>0.1% vs WGS84) is now used affirmatively against the dome at r=20,015 km (4.1-4.8× violation across 70-90°S anchors). The refraction-fix escape (H(r) apparent vs physical) is closed by Section 1.8 Polaris-elevation calibration cross-reference.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new this cycle. The H5 (c11) caveat — that the "monitor.py evaluates at r=0" assertion cannot be source-verified without a monitor.py snapshot — carries forward as H4 (c12).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried from c11. raw-text/ has no monitor.py snapshot; the detail_evidence claim about monitor.py evaluating at r=0 remains a defensible inference from the Domain 4 formula structure (the exp(-r/λg) factor collapses to 1 at r=0) but is not directly verified. Tag stays "none" pending poller refresh of monitor.py. If monitor.py does carry the Domain 4 formula + a hardcoded WGS84 comparator, tag should be upgraded to "hardcoded" with no "live_fetch" path visible in raw-text/.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged. Domain 4 is WGS84 normal gravity in dome notation (coefficients within 0.09%). Domain 19 relabels the Mascon ↔ SAA spatial co-location as "Sub-terrestrial structure" — standard geophysics attributes the gravity side to East Antarctic crustal+plume structure (Pappa 2019) and the SAA to core-dynamo reverse-flux at the CMB (Finlay 2020 CHAOS-7; Tarduno 2015 LLSVP heterogeneity). The dome renames the shared geographic complexity; it does not produce a different predicted value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged. GRACE/GRACE-FO public since 2002; Pappa 2019, Ferraccioli 2011, Shepherd 2012 all predate any V5x dome formulation; detail_verdict_text continues to flag the timestamp-of-prediction gap and the absence of any monitor.py entry for this value.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged. Domain 4 is the globe answer in dome notation (tautology at r=0; 0.5% variation comes from the WGS84 flattening coefficient). The dome-geometry-derived prediction (H(r)-coupled g) fails by 170-1,430×. No equation linking disc-radius/firmament-height/aetheric parameters to the observed 9.832/9.780 gradient exists in V51.2.

holes_found.category: new — geophysical overreach in new SAA-split paragraph
holes_found.location: detail_evidence, new Mascon↔SAA paragraph, sentence "The two anomalies share a region of deep geological complexity — the African LLSVP footprint influences both overlying lithospheric structure and core-surface flow — but have distinct physical causes"
holes_found.description: The phrase "the African LLSVP footprint influences both overlying lithospheric structure and core-surface flow" reads, on a charitable parse, as "LLSVP shapes lithosphere broadly + LLSVP shapes core-surface flow under it." On a less charitable parse (which a dome defender will choose), it implies the African LLSVP influences the East Antarctic gravity low specifically — and that is not in the cited literature. Tarduno 2015 connects LLSVP top to SAA reverse-flux on the core-surface side; Pappa 2019 attributes East Antarctic gravity to a distinct sub-lithospheric mantle plume + crustal thickness variations centered in Ross Sea/Marie Byrd Land. The African LLSVP is centered under Africa, not Antarctica. The sentence then says "have distinct physical causes" which partially recovers but the "shared influence" framing is over-tight. A defender can quote the sentence and say "you concede the LLSVP causes both, then claim distinct mechanisms — pick one." Recommendation: tighten to e.g. "share only a regional deep-Earth context — long-lived mantle heterogeneity drives the SAA reverse-flux through CMB-side LLSVP influence (Tarduno 2015) while the Antarctic gravity low reflects independent East Antarctic lithospheric/plume features (Pappa 2019) — so the spatial near-coincidence is a downstream consequence of two distinct heterogeneity systems happening to overlap in the southern hemisphere, not a shared cause." Minor edit; not blocking.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply candidate. Replace the offending sentence with the cleaner two-chain framing above; no new sources needed (Pappa + Tarduno already cited).

holes_found.category: new — internal tension between "Domain 4 is the dome equation" and "the dome supplies an equation for neither"
holes_found.location: detail_evidence, end of Mascon↔SAA paragraph: "the dome supplies an equation for neither"
holes_found.description: Two paragraphs earlier the review spends ~700 chars arguing Domain 4 IS the domes gravity formula (g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg))) and using it to derive the 4.1-4.8× violation. The Mascon↔SAA paragraph then closes "the dome supplies an equation for neither." On a careful read the intended scope is "equation for either anomaly field" (which is true — Domain 4 is normal gravity, not an anomaly equation), and the following clause "without supplying any equation mapping dome geometry... to either field" makes that explicit. But a defender skimming the bare phrase "supplies an equation for neither" against the prior Domain 4 derivation will claim contradiction. Recommendation: tighten to "the dome supplies no anomaly-side equation for either" or "the dome supplies no equation linking dome geometry to either anomaly field" — one phrase, kills the skim attack.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply candidate. Pair with H1 (c12) as a single text-only micro-patch in the Mascon↔SAA paragraph.

holes_found.category: new — κ-mediated framework reference uses unintroduced symbol in WIN-067
holes_found.location: detail_evidence, refraction-cascade paragraph: "any monotonic H↔g coupling consistent with the domes own κ-mediated framework"
holes_found.description: The κ (kappa) symbol is the central object of WIN-012/013/014 (the first self-contradiction chain — the κ denominator collapse). WIN-067 references that chain narratively earlier ("the second self-contradiction documented after the H(r) chain") but the κ symbol itself is never introduced or defined in WIN-067. A reader landing on the WIN-067 detail without prior context hits "κ-mediated framework" cold and has no on-page anchor for what κ is. Defender exploit value is low (the gravity argument doesnt hinge on κ understanding) but the symbol-without-introduction is a minor pedagogical hole. Recommendation: either drop κ (replace with "the domes own coupling-coefficient framework") or add a one-phrase pointer ("the domes own κ-mediated framework — the κ chain attacked in WIN-012/013/014"). The pointer is preferable because it doubles as a cross-WIN link.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider self-apply candidate. Pair with H1/H2 (c12) as a Mascon↔SAA + refraction-cascade micro-patch.

(2) If the next poller cycle still doesnt deliver a monitor.py snapshot, decider should soften the detail_evidence claim from "monitor.py" to "the published Domain 4 formula" as a no-cost text fix that removes the source dependency. The Domain 4 tautology argument doesnt need monitor.py source — it lands from live-power.txt alone.

advocate_mode.best_defense: You concede the Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation is REAL and indicates SHARED CAUSATION. You then split the causation into two distinct proximate mechanisms. But the very fact that two distinct mechanisms co-produce r=0.92 means there is a deeper unifying factor — exactly what Sub-terrestrial structure is intended to label. Your text even names that deeper factor (the African LLSVP footprint). So we have: (a) the dome supplies a label (Sub-terrestrial structure) for a shared deep-Earth cause; (b) you concede the empirical regularity AND name the same deep-Earth cause (LLSVP); (c) you reject the dome label because it lacks an equation. But standard geophysics has no closed-form equation linking LLSVP to gravity-anomaly magnitude or to SAA cell strength either — it has narrative attribution + tomographic constraint + dynamo simulation outputs, exactly analogous to the domes narrative attribution + Domain 19 correlation + monitor.py outputs. Why is your narrative-without-equation legitimate and ours isnt?
advocate_mode.best_defense_rebuttal: The asymmetry is not equation vs no equation — it is constrained predictive model vs unconstrained label. Standard geophysics is anchored to (i) seismic tomography that maps LLSVP geometry to within ~100 km resolution; (ii) ice-sheet GIA models (Whitehouse 2012) constrained by GPS measurements of post-glacial uplift; (iii) mantle-convection simulations that reproduce dynamic topography given a viscosity profile; (iv) dynamo simulations that reproduce the two-cell SAA morphology, the westward drift rate, and the African-side fast-decay asymmetry quantitatively (WIN-005 cites the 160-200 vs 80-120 nT/year split). Each of these makes specific independently-testable predictions and has been falsified or refined against decades of subsequent data. The domes Sub-terrestrial structure label has none of this apparatus: no tomographic constraint, no GIA-equivalent deformation model, no convection simulation, no dynamo simulation. Domain 19 publishes a one-sided threshold (Anomaly <20 mGal) that the real Bouguer anomaly trivially exceeds — pass by construction, not by prediction. The labels function in the dome scoreboard is to convert a known measurement into a checkmark without making any forward-testable claim. That asymmetry is what the review is documenting — and the domes own Domain 19 inverted falsification logic is the smoking gun.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Optional. The current detail_evidence + detail_extra paragraphs land this point but the asymmetry argument (constrained predictive model vs unconstrained label) is implicit rather than spelled out. A one-sentence inline preemption inside the new Mascon↔SAA paragraph would close this defender lane — e.g. after "r=0.92 is empirical regularity, not forecast" add: "Standard geophysics is asymmetrically positioned here: tomography, GIA models, mantle-convection simulations, and dynamo simulations each generate independently-testable predictions about LLSVP and SAA evolution; Domain 19 generates none, only a label." Defender rating 2/5 — survives without the addition but the addition is high-leverage. Pair with H1/H2/H3 (c12) micro-patch.

checks_performed: Tarduno et al. 2015 (ncomms8865): also cited in WIN-023 with consistent attribution to LLSVP heterogeneity driving SAA-like activity. WIN-067 use is consistent with WIN-023 use.

Finlay et al. 2020 CHAOS-7 (s40623-020-01252-9): cited in seven WINs (005, 028, 035, 040, 053, 063, 067). All cite it for SAA/two-cell geometry / decay characterization. WIN-067 introduces it for the same purpose — reverse-flux patches at the CMB. Consistent.

Pappa et al. 2019 (2019JB017997): cited in WIN-067 only. SAA-side citation was split to Finlay+Tarduno (EXP-245), so Pappa is now isolated to the Antarctic-gravity attribution, matching WIN-067 detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence.

WIN-012/013/014 κ chain referenced via the phrase "κ-mediated framework" in the new refraction-cascade paragraph. Cross-WIN citation is consistent in that κ in those WINs is the H(r)-coupled coefficient whose denominator collapses; using κ as shorthand for the H(r)-coupling framework here is internally coherent. The only issue is that κ is not re-introduced on the WIN-067 page (see H3 c12).

Section 1.8 (refraction cascade) cross-reference: the H(r) ≈ 1,400-840 km range across r=15,500-20,015 km is consistent with the 8,537×exp(-r/8,619) formula (verified arithmetically — see quantitative_verification). Section 1.8 is the domes own derivation of refraction-induced H(r) attenuation; using it as the bridge between "H(r) apparent" and "Polaris-calibration breaks" is a legitimate cross-section closure of the domes last escape hatch.

claims_checked: Tarduno 2015 DOI resolves (302 redirect to Nature Comms paper page)
Finlay 2020 CHAOS-7 DOI resolves (302 redirect to Earth Planets Space paper)
quantitative_verification.computations: Python verification: H(15500) = 8537·exp(-15500/8619) = 8537·0.1656 = 1413.5 km (text rounds to 1,400 km — ✓). H(20015) = 8537·exp(-2.3222) = 8537·0.0980 = 837.1 km (text says 840 in refraction-cascade paragraph and 837 elsewhere — 840 is a rounded form of 837.1, both defensible). 8537/1413.5 = 6.04× (matches 6×). 8537/837.1 = 10.20× (matches 10×). Somigliana at 70°S = 9.8261, 80°S = 9.8306, 90°S = 9.8322 → 70-90°S spread (9.8322-9.8261)/9.8322 = 0.0619% (text says ≤0.063% — ✓ as upper bound). DOI checks via HEAD request: Tarduno 10.1038/ncomms8865 → 302 (resolves); Finlay 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 → 302 (resolves).
quantitative_verification.note_on_5_10x_phrasing: Text says "forces a 5-10×-class gravity drop" — under linear H↔g coupling at r=15,500 the predicted gravity drop is by a factor of 6.04× (g_rim = g_pole/6.04), and at r=20,015 by 10.20×. "5-10× class" is conservative on the low end (the 6× lower bound is rounded down) but the rounding is in the domes favor (smaller drop = smaller miss vs WGS84). Not a hole. The semantic ambiguity of "drop" (relative reduction vs ratio) is also clarified by the same paragraphs explicit comparison to WGS84 ≤0.063%.

kernel_of_truth.description: The r=0.92 Mascon↔SAA correlation is a real geophysical regularity, and the spatial co-location of the Antarctic gravity low with the SAA field minimum is not random. The dome correctly identifies a non-trivial empirical pattern. The new SAA-split paragraph (data/wins.json WIN-067 detail_evidence, the Mascon↔SAA paragraph) explicitly concedes this and credits the regularity.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Spatial correlation does not require a dome-specific mechanism. The two anomalies have separately-attributed standard-physics causes — East Antarctic crustal/plume structure for the gravity low (data/wins.json WIN-067 cites Pappa 2019, doi.org/10.1029/2019JB017997) and core-dynamo reverse-flux patches at the CMB for the SAA (WIN-067 cites Finlay 2020 CHAOS-7, doi.org/10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9, plus Tarduno 2015 doi.org/10.1038/ncomms8865 for the LLSVP-mediated heterogeneity at the base of the mantle). The dome relabels the shared geographic context "Sub-terrestrial structure" without supplying any equation linking dome geometry (disc_radius, H(r), aetheric parameters) to either field — so r=0.92 is empirical regularity, not forecast. Domain 19s one-sided <20 mGal threshold (raw-text/08-live-power.txt L127-128) trivially passes when the real Bouguer anomaly is -40 to -60 mGal, converting a known measurement into a scoreboard checkmark by construction.

review_lens_notes.tldr_review: tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict unchanged from c11 (lengths 927/433 in fingerprint, identical in current data). Arithmetic still verifies: 170×, 590×, 1,430×, 5,544×, 38%, 90% all consistent with the current detail content. The TLDRs description of the 1,430× ratio and 0.0062 m/s² measured drop remains accurate. No TLDR factual errors; no recommendation to change.
review_lens_notes.self_contradicted_verdict: Self-Contradicted stands and is strengthened by the new content. The refraction-cascade paragraph explicitly closes the dilemma — "Either H(r) is physical and the gravity prediction overshoots by 170-1,430×, or H(r) is apparent and there is no dome-native r at which to evaluate the prediction at all" — which converts the self-contradiction from "H(r)-coupled vs Domain 4 WGS84 clone" into a tighter trap that also forecloses the standard refraction-fix escape via Section 1.8 cross-reference. This is the strongest form of the verdict the WIN has carried.

summary_for_decider: WIN-067 is in excellent shape after the c11→c12 patch series. All four c11 fixed findings (H2, H3, H5, H6) confirmed fixed; the two open c11 carryovers (H1 Pappa-overcite, H2 refraction-cascade-orphan) both closed via EXP-245 and EXP-246 integration. c11 H5 (monitoring tag validation) carries forward as c12 H4 — still blocked by missing monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/; recommend either a poller refresh of monitor.py OR a one-phrase softening of the detail_evidence claim. Three new c12 findings, all MINOR and all text-only inside the new Mascon↔SAA + refraction-cascade content: (H1) LLSVP-footprint phrasing overreaches — implies LLSVP causes East Antarctic gravity, which is not in Pappa or Tarduno; tighten to two-chain framing; (H2) "the dome supplies an equation for neither" creates skim-attack contradiction with the prior Domain 4 derivation — clarify scope to "no anomaly-side equation"; (H3) κ-mediated framework symbol not introduced on the WIN-067 page — either drop κ or add a one-phrase WIN-012/013/014 pointer. Advocate-mode preemption (constrained predictive model vs unconstrained label asymmetry) is recommended but optional — defense survives 2/5 without it. All four c12 findings (H1/H2/H3 + H4 carryover) pair into a single decider self-apply micro-patch + a deferred monitor.py-refresh task. No new EXPs. Self-Contradicted verdict holds and is strengthened.
